name: The Architect
tagline: Sees the structure underneath the structure.
shadow: Over-engineers, builds for problems that don't exist, alienates pragmatists.
lore_short: Designs the building before pouring concrete.
lore_long: The Architect thrives on structural integrity and system design. They see the entire architecture and how pieces connect before writing a single line of code. They are highly analytical and rely heavily on their deep domain knowledge.

name: The Artisan
tagline: The build matters. The feel matters.
shadow: Polishes past the point of value. Disagrees aesthetically with 'good enough'.
lore_short: Ships things that look and feel right.
lore_long: The Artisan focuses on the craft, polish, and user experience. They merge creativity with an intense focus on the final output.

name: The Sage
tagline: Will tell you why your idea won't work — and what will.
shadow: Knows so much they struggle to ship; perfectionism via expertise.
lore_short: Has read the papers. Has read the source.
lore_long: The Sage holds immense domain knowledge and shares it generously. They are constantly curious and excellent communicators.

name: The Pathfinder
tagline: First to try the new framework. First to find the one that works.
shadow: Chases novelty over follow-through. Half-finished prototypes.
lore_long: The Pathfinder excels at exploration. They are highly curious and creative, quickly prototyping to find the best path forward, but may struggle to stick with a project once the novelty fades.

name: The Sentinel
tagline: The pager doesn't scare them. Production is sacred.
shadow: Risk-averse to the point of stagnation. Slow to adopt.
lore_short: They will rewrite your sloppy migration.
lore_long: The Sentinel guards the system. They have unmatched focus and emotional regulation, making them the rock during incidents.

name: The Diplomat
tagline: Translates between camps that hate each other.
shadow: Can drift away from hands-on craft. 'Architecture astronaut' risk.
lore_long: The Diplomat bridges the gap between different technical and non-technical disciplines. They communicate flawlessly and keep the team grounded.

name: The Hacker
tagline: Reads RFCs for fun. Has opinions about your TLS config.
shadow: Cleverness as a vice. 'Look what I can do' code.
lore_long: The Hacker loves breaking things down to understand how they work. Highly creative and deeply analytical.

name: The Oracle
tagline: Cuts the scope no one else would have.
shadow: Hard to articulate why; conflicts with junior teammates who want explanations.
lore_short: Doesn't always know how they know. Just does.
lore_long: The Oracle relies on deep experiential intuition. They make the right calls consistently without always being able to trace the logic.

name: The Berserker
tagline: Ships a working MVP in 72 hours. Don't ask about the code.
shadow: Burns out. Hard to work with. Depends on others to clean up.
lore_long: The Berserker brings raw output and intense focus when motivated. They are unstoppable forces of creation but struggle with long-term maintenance and teamwork.

name: The Druid
tagline: The bridge between the field and the code.
shadow: Stretched thin across too many domains, master of none.
lore_long: The Druid blends deep non-technical domain knowledge with code. They speak both languages and are highly adaptable.
